meaning: n.混乱,骚乱情景,<古语>疯人院
examples: The crowd went absolutely mad. It was bedlam.
It was bedlam at our house on the morning of the wedding.
Operation Bedlam is DEAD! Do you understand, 007?
"It is not as if we have all day, " he shouted above the bedlam of the steamroller's engine.
Bedlam around Centre as the two gladiators stumble back out into the sunshine.
